task: ring mesh (N=12); class 0 = coherent twist R(theta*i)u (anisotropic), class 1 = isotropic; zero-mean by construction (raw mean = chance)
pipeline: RotorMeshNet (deep learned rotors + mesh mix) -> readout -> logistic; trained by closed-form chain (BCE->logistic->readout backward->RotorMeshNet::backward->bivecs), NO autograd

fd_gate.verdict: PASS (<5e-2) — end-to-end gradient verified, training not a phantom

note: DOUBLE DISSOCIATION: deep+entropy 0.759 wins; removing EITHER depth (->0.561) OR the entropy readout (->0.542) collapses to ~chance (0.544 both-off). Both depth AND arrangement(entropy) load-bearing => the deep holonomy net LEARNS useful features THROUGH DEPTH readable only by the entropy (arrangement) readout. HONEST: trained by GD on the closed-form holonomy gradient (stepping stone), NOT yet the instantaneous entropy-feedback rule; entropy is the READOUT/objective, training is supervised BCE. AUROC 0.759 above chance not saturated (hard zero-mean task); loss decrease gentle.

convergence_deep_entropy_seed0.note: held-out AUROC vs #passes. ONE pass already 0.728 (near-instantaneous, mostly architecture + linear probe over random deep rotors; vs shallow 0.561). The committed 2x2 (lr=0.05) was UNDERTRAINED -> ~0.74 (near the one-pass floor). With lr=2.0 iterative training refines to 0.90. So: the deep-holonomy+entropy ARCHITECTURE is discriminative near-instantly; ROTOR-weight learning (0.73->0.90) is still ITERATIVE GD. The pure one-shot instantaneous ROTOR rule remains open.

optimizers_deep_entropy_seed0.note: ADAPTIVE MOMENTUM (Adam, reusing adam_step) DRAMATICALLY accelerates the rotor-weight learning AND raises the ceiling: Adam reaches 0.87 in 10 passes (GD needs ~100) and 0.947 at 200 (GD 0.902). Nesterov helps (0.83 by ~20, ceiling 0.865) but less than Adam — adaptive PER-PARAMETER scaling is the lever (rotor-bivec grads have very different scales). This is PLAIN EUCLIDEAN Adam on the Cayley-chart bivecs = step 1 (cheap baseline); the CLIFFORD (parallel-transport momentum by inverse rotor) + ENTROPY (natural-gradient preconditioner) refinements are next. Still ITERATIVE (~10 passes, not one shot) but few-shot = practical near-instantaneous.

competitiveness_fair_fight_5seed_median.note: HONEST NEGATIVE: on THIS task the deep holonomy net does NOT earn its complexity. The TRIVIAL raw-field entropy + logistic (NO holonomy net, ~2 params) BEATS the deep holonomy net (0.944 vs 0.894). The coherent-twist vs isotropic classes are already separated by the RAW field's covariance eigen-entropy (coherent = 2D-spread structured cov, isotropic = 3D-spread) -- so the deep rotor 'un-twisting' is unnecessary and here slightly COUNTERPRODUCTIVE (random rotors + mesh mix partly scramble the cleanly-visible coherence). MLPs (0.69/0.67) trail both -- confirming the ENTROPY readout (a 2nd-order stat) is the lever, not depth. This RETRACTS the competitiveness implication of F-HOLO-1: the double dissociation is internally real (depth+entropy beat the holonomy net's OWN ablations) but against the RIGHT external baseline the whole apparatus loses. Evaluation-metric-integrity lesson: I optimized under a ceiling (0.944 trivial) that a 2-param baseline already exceeds.
